name: Erwin, C. Blackstone
motto: "We have had an extraordinary man here."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Gordon Hall Boarding Club 1915-1916

name: Sandford, Lundie Boone
motto: "He is happy whose circumstances suit his temper; but he is more excellent who can suit his temper to any circumstances."

name: Belton, O'Neal Bourn
motto: "I want to grow as beautiful as God meant me to be."

name: James, Edwin Bramlette
motto: "They say thou art a lawyer in disguise; 'Tis well, I would not suspect it otherwise."
clubs: Gordon Hall Board of Control 1914-15, and 1915-16

name: Brooks, Rodney L.
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"

name: Buntin, Robert R.
motto: "Studious of ease, and fond of humble things."

name: Carr, Oscar Clark
motto: "His life was gentle, and the elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up and say to all the world: 'This is a gentleman.'"

name: Cassibry, Napoleon L.
motto: "A better fellow to walk with down life's highway is seldom found."
clubs: Chairman Board of Control Gordon Hall

name: Cleveland, T. B.
motto: "Thy face is the index of a noble mind."

name: Colbert, C. E.
motto: "Love in my heart as idly burns, As fire in antique Roman urns."

name: Crawford, Ivy Winfred
motto: "Had he been caught when young he might have been tamed."

name: Crook, Robert Hall
motto: "My only books were woman's looks, And folly's all they've taught me."

name: Crosby, Victor
motto: "Maidens, beware; this Lord has his eyes upon you."

name: Curlee, John Mcferrin
motto: "I have immortal longings in me."

name: Cutrer, John Clark
motto: "His words, like so many nimble and airy servitors, trip about him at command."

name: Duncan, Sallie Isabella
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned To warn, to comfort, and command."

name: Dunn, Thomas Dewitt
motto: "Though I am splenetic and rash, Yet I have something in me dangerous."

name: Farley, Nannie
motto: "None know thee but to love, Nor name thee but to praise."

name: George, Jesse Alexander
motto: "I always get the better when I argue alone."

name: Gillespie, William Meek
motto: "And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew, How one small head could carry all he knew."

name: Good, Robert
motto: "As mild a mannered man As ever scuttled a ship, or cut a throat."
major: M. A., University, B. S. Degree, with distinction

name: Hairston, William Gray
motto: "I rise with the lark (?)"

name: Hargis, Arthur Chandler
motto: "I let the world wagge, and take mine ease."

name: Harris, J. Harley
motto: "I am as sober as a judge."
clubs: Board of Control Gordon Hall Boarding Club 1915-16

name: Hume, Annie Fulton
motto: "Loyal-hearted, strong of mind, A finer girl nowhere you'll find."

name: Johnson, Daniel Rupert
motto: "He draweth out the thread of his verbosity finer than the staple of his argument."

name: Kyle, Albert Sidney Jr.
motto: "I never knew so young a body with so old a head."

name: Lauderdale, Joe
motto: "Take-it-easy and live-long are brothers."

name: Long, Mary Bibb
motto: "She is pretty to walk with, and witty to talk with, and pleasant too to think on."

name: Mccorkle, Fred Stewart
motto: "Beside, 'tis known he could speak Greek As natural as pigs squeak; That Latin was no more difficile Than to a blackbird 'tis to whistle."

name: Mccoy, Oliver Council
motto: "The mildest manners and the gentlest heart."

name: Adlia, McInnis
motto: "And when a lady's in the case, You know all other things give place."

name: Hugh Carroll, McLeod
motto: "Much study is a weariness of the flesh."

name: Thomas Herron, Mitchell
motto: "Linked sweetness long drawn out."
clubs: Secretary Hermaean Literary Society, Third Term, 1915
Chairman Membership Committee Y. M. C. A. Cabinet

name: Charles Hardin, Murphey
motto: "I wept when I was born, And every day shows why."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Scribblers' Club 1915-16
description: Len Martin: "That's the psychology of it."

name: Murphy, William E.
motto: "Life is as tedious as a twice-told tale."

name: Nason, Richard Henry
motto: "Six hours in sleep, in law's grave study six, Four spend in prayer, the rest on Nature fix."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Students' Co-Operative Boarding Club 1914-15
Inter-Society Debater first term 1915, Phi Sigma

name: Oliver, Robert Lewellyn
motto: "I will a round, unvarnished tale deliver of my whole course of love."

name: Patrick, Ellis William
motto: "If hot air were music I would be a whole brass band."
clubs: Anniversarian of Phi Sigma Literary Society 1915-16

name: Rauch, John Melvin
motto: "The love he bore to learning was at fault."

name: Reed, William McNeil
motto: "Uneasy lies the head that wears a crown."—Or gets out an Annual.

name: Rhodes, Lillian Stokes
motto: "She hath an artist's skill, a student's knowledge, and a soul's glad life."

name: Robinson, Walter W.
motto: "Oh, bliss is mine, for into my joyless life a woman has come."

name: Shackleford, Robert Hoffman
motto: "The time I've lost in wooing, In watching and pursuing The light that lies in woman's eyes, Has been my heart's undoing."

name: Sherrod, Julian
motto: "A fly sat upon the axle of a chariot and exclaimed, 'What a dust I do raise.'"

name: Smith, Otis Forrest
motto: "Now, in the name of all the gods at once, Upon what meat doth this our Caesar feed That he is grown so great?"
clubs: Member, Students' Co-Operative Boarding Club Board of Control 1914-15

name: Stovall, Arthur
motto: "In the spring (summer, autumn, and winter too, for Arthur) a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love."
description: Coach Robbins: "Spit on your hands, men."

name: Sweat, Noah Spurgeon
motto: "I lay me down to sleep with little care, Whether my waking finds me here or there."

name: Wooten, Walter Hardin
motto: "I never dare to be as funny as I am."

name: Leavell, Leonard O.
description: From out the haven, the sparkling moonlit bay, Which in youth they have learned to love, Past the familiar buoys which mark the channel On, on, over and across the bar Into the tempting, shimmering expanse of destiny They go! —H. M. B. Prof. Schutz: "I am going to get this class a novel in a few days."

name: Albin, Edward Roby Jr.
motto: "Call not that man wretched, who whatever else he suffers, has a child to love."

name: Anderson, Cephus A.
motto: "I am resolved to grow fat and look young until forty."

name: Bean, George
motto: "Let's have wine, woman, mirth, and laughter, Sermons and classes some day after."

name: Bowdre, Paul H.
motto: "What a fine man hath thy tailor made thee!"
description: "I've got to work a problem in graphic statics."

name: Boydston, Reuben W.
motto: "Another good farmer spoiled."

name: Brown, Linda Rives
motto: "Marriage is a desperate thing."
clubs: Member of Y. W. C. A. Cabinet 1915-16

name: Bryan, Harry Meredith
motto: "Fire in each eye, and papers in each hand, He roves, recites, and maddens 'round the land."
description: Chaimers Potter: "Pass the sweet potatoes, please."

name: Carter, E. Bunyan
motto: "Not over serious, not too gay, But a rare good fellow."
clubs: Member First Board of Control of "The Mississippian," and suggested name of the paper

name: Colson, Joseph G.
motto: "Though modest, on his unembarrassed brow Nature has written—Gentleman."

name: Dawson, D. A.
motto: "Be gone, my cases, I give you to the wind."
clubs: Football, Basketball, and Baseball University of Tenn., 1912-13-14

name: Dear, W. C.
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous in their wisdom."

name: Gathings, Joseph R.
motto: "O, let not woman's weapons, water drops, Stain my man's cheeks."

name: James Edward Hamlin, Ll. B.
motto: "He trudged along, unknowing what he sought, And whistled as he went, for want of thought."

name: Francis Higdon Harper, Ll. B.
motto: "Gather ye rosebuds while ye may, Old Time is still a-flying."
clubs: Senator from Grenada and Yalobusha Counties, Miss.

name: Daniel Rupert Johnson, Ll. B.
motto: "Could we forbear dispute and practice love, We should agree as angels do above."

name: Lewis Barrett Jones, Ll. B.
motto: "I must have liberty Withal, as large a charter as the wind, To blow on whom I please."
major: A. B. Millsaps College; Ll. B.
description: Pitt Stone: "Well I don't know about that; I can see a few objections already."

name: Leonard, L. Martin
motto: "As jolly, as handsome, as solid as any since creation, Altogether a good president of the 'Y. M. C. A. Association,' A loyal supporter of the 'Sa'day E'nin' Post'; A regular 'Cruso' with his voice—almost."
clubs: Chairman Music Y. M. C. A. 1914-15
Winner of prize best work in Moot Court, 1914-15

name: Bunch, Frank Marion
motto: "For he by geometric scale, Could take the size of pots of ale."

name: Davis, Louis Irving
motto: "As idle as a painted ship upon a painted ocean."

name: Gibson, Robert Clayton
motto: "A good tall fellow!"

name: Kirkwood, John William
motto: "Go not indiscriminately to classes."

name: Pridgen, Ira Achiellius
motto: "Heaven bless thee, Thou hast the sweetest face I ever looked on."

name: DeWitt Talmage Allred, B. S., M. D.
motto: "I have a soul that like an ample shield can take in all, and verge enough for more."

name: William Henry Anderson, B. S., M. D.
motto: "Let us do or die."
clubs: President Board of Control Students' Co-Operative Boarding Club

name: Jonathan Jesse Armstrong, B. S., M. D.
motto: "A friendship that makes the least noise is very often the most useful."

name: Fred Buckley Bass, M. D.
motto: "Live man, well man, dead man, stiff, Dig 'em up, cut 'em up, what's the dif? Humorous, tumorous, blood and gore, I'll be an M. D. in two years more."
description: R. L. Oliver: "Writing to Mrs. R. L. Oliver."

name: Bethea, Ernest S.
motto: "If he had any faults he has left us in doubt."

name: Black, James Bailey
motto: "An honest mind is not in the power of a dishonest."
clubs: Board of Control Co-Operative Boarding Club
President Phi Sigma Literary Society, Second Term, 1916

name: Bowlin, Robert Leslie
motto: "If there be, or ever were one such, It's past the size of dreaming."

name: Brunson, Clyde W.
motto: "His smile doth beam, Even as the sun upon a summer's day."

name: Carey Cheek, M. D.
motto: "Discretion shows the better part of valour."

name: Allan Colleette, M. D.
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man."
clubs: Assistant in Departments of Physiology and Histology

name: Elliott E. Flowers, M. D.
motto: "In manners gentle, of affection mild, In wit a man, simplicity a child."

name: Robert M. Freeman, M. D.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: Miss Aline Ray: "Oh, girls, I'm in love!"

name: Hampton, George Gardner
motto: "No solemn sanctimonious face I pull."

name: Kellum, Henry Jack
motto: "A quiet, thoughtful, good, sincere lad."
major: M. D., Student Assistant in Pharmacology

name: Lowe, James Osborne
motto: "I had rather men would ask why my statue is not set up, than why it is."

name: Newsome, Bruce
motto: "Thank you sweetly."

name: Rice, James C.
motto: "Surer to prosper than prosperity could have assured us."
clubs: Inter-Collegiate Debater against Millsaps College from Phi Sigma

name: Robinson, Walter Westbrook
motto: "Night after night he sat and bleared his eyes with books."

name: Rowland, Peter Whitman Jr.
motto: "A voice more thrilling ne'er was heard, In springtime from the mocking-bird."

name: Stacy, Archer Jackson
motto: "O'er books I consume the midnight oil."

name: Atkinson, Walter Bailey
motto: "My drugs will affect great cures."

name: Ben H. Donald, Jr.
motto: "My feet are heavy now but on I go."

name: Jack D. Dyre
motto: "It is so soon that I am done for, I wonder what I was begun for."

name: Rufus Noble Eley
motto: "Life is short and thou art long."

name: Charles W. Fulton
motto: "Now my task is smoothly done; I can fly or I can run."
description: Miss Mamie Morgan: "Hun, I'd like to, but I've got to study."

name: Hairston, William C.
motto: "He never flunked and he never lied; I reckon he never knewed how."

name: Huff, James Hatton
motto: "God made him and then lost the pattern."

name: Kendall, Samuel Watts
motto: "Pete' Dear: 'Goin' 'cross the railroad tonight.'"
clubs: Alternate Ole Miss Representative Chautauqua Oratorical Contest 1914-15, and alternate Ole Miss Representative M. I. O. A. 1915-16

name: Lummus, Floyd Alvero
motto: "His various cares in one great point combine, This business of his life—that is to dine."

name: Northrop, Abner Knight
motto: "That fellow seems to me to possess but one idea, and that is a wrong one."

name: Riley, Harris D.
motto: "Rare on earth is such constant prep found."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Junior Pharmacy Class 1914-15

name: Roberts, George C.
motto: "The noblest Roman of them all."

name: Ford, Tom Ruble
motto: "The march of the human mind is slow."

name: Rozell, Lloyd Russell
motto: "Cursed be he who moves my bones."

name: Percy, Isaac Sandman
motto: "There is some ill a-brewing toward my rest, For I dream of money bags to-night."

name: Kelly, Ernest Skinner
motto: "Ez soshubble ez a basket o' kittens."
description: Ike Hollingsworth: "The Bible says that men love darkness better than day; guess that's why Durfey loves Knight so well."

name: Thompson, E. Ray
motto:
"In alle the ordres foure is none that kan
So muchel of daliaunce and fair language."

name: Triplett, Wallace N.
motto:
"Let the world slide, let the world go;
A fig for care, and a fig for woe."

name: Ward, John Marion
motto: "Oh wearisome condition of humanity."

name: Whittington, Curtis Alonzo
motto: "A lion among the ladies is a dangerous thing."
